fondo_rating.scoring_notes: GOLD wave 2026-07-24 (batch C, sol-reviewed): DOWN-SCORED TO FLOOR, NOT DELETED. Live research (codex/gpt-5.6-sol web search) found no credible official or independent record of an event actually named 'Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo' at Lake Saiko or anywhere in the Fuji Five Lakes region. The profile's vitals line up, piece by piece, with at least four DIFFERENT, unrelated real events, though no single source ties them together under this name: (1) '120.7km/1,500 riders/6 feed zones' matches Mt.FUJI Eco Cycling 2016's 120km category (1,500-rider cap, six documented aid stations at Yamanakako, not Saiko); (2) 'Fuji Panorama' as a name belongs to the real but unrelated 2018 Fuji Panorama Time Trial (a 3.776km hill TT with 170m elevation change, held alongside Fuji-san Long Ride — nothing like a 120km gran fondo); (3) '1,800m climbing/October' matches later Fuji-san 1-Lap Cycling (Fujiichi) editions; (4) the '8-hour cutoff' matches the unrelated 2023 Monte Fuji Granfondo (Shizuoka side, 140km/2,500m, 30-rider field). All 14 dimensions and cultural_impact set to floor value (1/0) because no real event was found to score — treat this as an unverified profile apparently assembled from several separate Fuji-area events, not as proof of deliberate fabrication. Original score before this wave: 63 (migrated from gravel-race-automation, pre-recalculation) — that score should never have been assigned without a verified event behind it.

course_description.character: No event-specific course can be described responsibly — there is no verified 'Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo' to describe. The Fuji Five Lakes region does host real, currently-running long rides, most notably Suruga Bank presents Fuji-san 1-Lap Cycling (Fujiichi), a supported, noncompetitive circuit of the five lakes with a confirmed October 25, 2026 date. That is a genuinely different event and should not be conflated with this profile's claimed vitals.

history.origin_story: No founding history could be verified because no event under this name was found. The vitals appear to be a composite drawn from at least four unrelated real Fuji-area events (see scoring_notes) rather than a single event's actual history.

biased_opinion.verdict: Unverified — No Confirmed Event Under This Name
biased_opinion.summary: No credible official or independent record confirms that 'Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo' exists at Lake Saiko or anywhere in the Fuji Five Lakes region. Live research found this profile's vitals line up, piece by piece, with at least four different, unrelated real events: Mt.FUJI Eco Cycling 2016 (matches the 120km/1,500-rider/6-feed-zone figures, actually based at Yamanakako, not Saiko), the 2018 Fuji Panorama Time Trial (a 3.776km hill TT, matches the name, nothing like a gran fondo), later Fuji-san 1-Lap Cycling/Fujiichi editions (matches the 1,800m/October figures), and the 2023 Monte Fuji Granfondo (matches the 8-hour cutoff, a 30-rider Shizuoka-side event). No source ties any of this together under one real event.
weaknesses: No verifiable event exists under this name — this is not a race you can register for or plan travel around
The claimed vitals are a composite of facts borrowed from at least four unrelated real events, not a coherent single-event profile
No official site, organizer, or registration record exists for 'Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo' under any spelling or language checked
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Do not treat this as a real, bookable event. If you want a genuine Fuji Five Lakes long ride, look at Suruga Bank presents Fuji-san 1-Lap Cycling (Fujiichi) — a confirmed, currently-running supported circuit of all five lakes with a real October 2026 date.

final_verdict.one_liner: No verifiable event exists under this name — vitals appear assembled from several unrelated real Fuji-area rides.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No. This event could not be verified to exist under this name anywhere in the Fuji Five Lakes region.
final_verdict.alternatives: Suruga Bank presents Fuji-san 1-Lap Cycling (Fujiichi) — a real, confirmed supported long ride circling all five Fuji lakes, October 25, 2026, organized by Roots Sports Japan.

eligibility.notes: No credible evidence supports a 'Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo' at Lake Saiko or anywhere in the Fuji Five Lakes region. Live web research found the profile's vitals trace to a composite of at least four unrelated real events (Mt.FUJI Eco Cycling 2016, the 2018 Fuji Panorama Time Trial, Fuji-san 1-Lap Cycling/Fujiichi, and the 2023 Monte Fuji Granfondo) — see fondo_rating.scoring_notes for the full breakdown. Status held at 'unknown' rather than 'defunct' because there's no evidence this specific event ever existed to begin with. Score floored to 20/T4 pending human review; recommend considering removal from the catalog entirely, or replacing it with a freshly researched profile for the real Fuji-san 1-Lap Cycling (Fujiichi) event.

catalog_flags.status_note: Unverified/apparently-assembled profile — flagging for a human call on whether this belongs in the catalog at all, or should be replaced with a freshly researched profile for the real Fuji-san 1-Lap Cycling (Fujiichi) event (confirmed Oct 25, 2026, Yamanashi/Shizuoka, organizer Roots Sports Japan).
